items:
**Mathematical induction**: Prove $P(n_0)$; prove 
 $P(k) \Rightarrow P(k+1)$; conclude $P(n)$ for all $n \geq n_0$.

**Strong induction**: The inductive hypothesis assumes 
 $P(j)$ for all $j \leq k$, not just $P(k)$.

**Recursive definitions**: Define functions or sets using 
 base cases and recursive rules.

**Recurrence relations**: Equations defining sequences in 
 terms of previous terms.

**Structural induction**: Proves properties of recursively 
 defined structures.

**Well-ordering principle**: Every nonempty subset of $\N$ 
 has a smallest element.

content.text:
Prove by strong induction: Every integer $n \geq 8$ can be 
written as $n = 3a + 5b$ for non-negative integers $a, b$.

Give a recursive definition for the sequence 
$1, 3, 7, 15, 31, \ldots$

Prove by structural induction: In any rooted binary tree, 
the number of leaves is one more than the number of internal nodes 
with two children.
